title: WOS API Correspondence
description: Public API resource schema for correspondence messages and delivery-template renderings. A CorrespondenceMessage is a single addressable inbound or outbound communication associated with a WOS workflow process; a CorrespondenceRendering is the result of rendering a delivery-sidecar notification template into a draft message body. Greenfield per ADR 0082; closed taxonomies project the WOS delivery sidecar (https://wos-spec.org/schemas/delivery/1.0) into the public API surface without redefinition.

WorkflowUrn.description: Absolute URI of the WOS workflow document this correspondence is associated with. Identifies the delivery sidecar that supplies templates and entry-template metadata.

MessageDirection.description: Direction of the correspondence message relative to the agency. Closed projection of the delivery sidecar `direction` enum (https://wos-spec.org/schemas/delivery/1.0#/$defs/EntryTemplate/properties/direction).

MessageChannel.description: Communication channel for the correspondence message. Reserved literals project the delivery sidecar `channel` enum (in-person, phone, mail, email, portal, fax). Vendor-prefixed extensions are namespaced per ADR 0082 D-12.

CorrespondencePartyRole.description: Role of the party who sent or received the correspondence. Closed projection of the delivery sidecar `correspondenceRole` enum (https://wos-spec.org/schemas/delivery/1.0#/$defs/EntryTemplate/properties/correspondenceRole).

MessageStatus.description: Delivery lifecycle of the correspondence message. `draft|queued|sent|delivered|failed|cancelled` apply to outbound messages; `received|acknowledged` apply to inbound messages. Closed enum with no extension seam; new states require a schema major bump.

MessageProducer.description: Producer family that authored or ingested the correspondence message. Reserved literals describe the canonical sources the WOS reference server emits or projects; vendor-prefixed extensions per ADR 0082 D-12.

RenderingCategory.description: Notification template category resolved during rendering. Closed projection of the delivery sidecar `NotificationTemplate.category` enum (https://wos-spec.org/schemas/delivery/1.0#/$defs/NotificationTemplate/properties/category).

DeliveryChannelKind.description: Outbound delivery channel declared for a rendered message, projecting the delivery sidecar `deliveryChannels` enum. Distinct from `MessageChannel`, which classifies the underlying communication channel for both inbound and outbound traffic.

ContentRef.description: Claim-check pointer to the actual correspondence payload. The payload is never embedded in the API resource; this URI MUST resolve through the bundle service or an authenticated agency document store. Format-restricted to absolute URI to prevent open-string `Record<string, unknown>` style escape hatches.

TemplateRef.description: Identifier of a delivery-sidecar template (entry template id for inbound logging, notification template key for outbound rendering). Resolution happens against the workflow's delivery sidecar at the server.

oneOf.description: String variable value substituted verbatim into the rendered template.
Numeric variable value (floating-point). Renderers convert to canonical decimal form.
Integer variable value. Renderers convert to canonical decimal form.
Boolean variable value. Renderers emit `true` or `false` unless the template formats explicitly.

oneOf.$comment: why-null: explicit null is a legitimate variable value distinct from absence; an absent variable raises `missingVariables`, while null renders to the empty string.
oneOf.description: Explicit null variable value. Renders to the empty string; distinct from omitting the variable entirely.

items.description: String element of a multi-value variable (e.g., reason codes).

oneOf.description: Multi-value string variable (e.g., reason codes). Renderers join elements per template formatting.

RenderingVariableValue.description: Permitted JSON-primitive value types for a rendering variable: string, number, integer, boolean, null, or array of strings. Nested objects are intentionally forbidden so callers cannot smuggle arbitrary case-state shapes through the rendering context.

RenderingVariable.description: Single named variable supplied to the renderer. Closed shape with no `Record<string, unknown>` escape hatch.

name.description: Variable name matched against `{{name}}` placeholders in the resolved template. Constrained identifier shape to keep the rendering surface narrow.

value.description: Variable value. Closed JSON-primitive set; nested objects are not expressible by design.

RenderedSection.description: Rendered section of a correspondence message. Mirrors the section ordering of the source delivery template after condition evaluation.

id.description: Source section identifier from the delivery template.

title.description: Optional section heading rendered above the body.

contentType.description: Closed content-type taxonomy projected from the delivery template `TemplateSection.contentType` (https://wos-spec.org/schemas/delivery/1.0#/$defs/TemplateSection/properties/contentType).

body.description: Rendered section body with placeholders substituted.
examples: Your application for SNAP has been denied.

CorrespondenceMessage.description: Public WOS correspondence message: a single inbound or outbound communication associated with a workflow process. The `contentRef` field is a claim-check pointer; payload bytes never travel through this API resource.

id.description: Stable public URN for this correspondence message.

processId.description: Workflow process URN this correspondence belongs to.

workflowUrl.description: Workflow URI used to resolve the delivery sidecar templates referenced by this message.

templateRef.description: Delivery-sidecar template identifier this message conforms to.

direction.description: Direction of this message relative to the agency.

partyRole.description: Role of the party who sent or received this message.

status.description: Current delivery lifecycle of this message.

producer.description: Producer family that authored or ingested this message.

subject.description: Optional subject line for outbound messages or extracted subject for inbound messages.

summary.description: Required human-readable one-line summary of this correspondence event for case timelines and audit logs.
examples: Adverse benefits decision notice mailed to applicant

contentRef.description: Claim-check pointer to the message payload (rendered notice PDF, scanned mail image, email blob, transcript, etc.).

renderingId.description: When this message was produced from a delivery-template rendering, the rendering's URN. Absent for raw-logged inbound messages.

deliveryChannels.description: Outbound delivery channels the agency intends to use for this message. Omitted for inbound messages.

relatedTaskId.description: Optional WOS task URN this correspondence relates to.

relatedNotificationId.description: Optional WOS notification URN this correspondence was projected into the user feed under.

actorRef.description: Actor responsible for sending or recording this message when one applies.

occurredAt.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp when the correspondence event occurred (mailed, received, phone-call ended).

recordedAt.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp when the server recorded this correspondence message.

expiresAt.description: Optional retention horizon. UTC RFC 3339 timestamp or the literal `never` for indefinite retention (ADR 0082 D-10).

CorrespondenceListOptions.description: Query fields for listing correspondence messages.

processId.description: When present, restrict results to this workflow process.

direction.description: When present, restrict results to this direction.

status.description: When present, restrict results to this delivery status.

channel.description: When present, restrict results to this communication channel.

partyRole.description: When present, restrict results to this party role.

cursor.description: Opaque resume token returned by the previous page (ADR 0082 D-7).

limit.description: Maximum number of correspondence messages requested for this page (ADR 0082 D-7).

items.description: Correspondence messages in this page, ordered by `occurredAt` descending unless the server documents otherwise.

cursor.description: Opaque token for the next page. Omitted when hasMore is false.

hasMore.description: True when another page is available from the server.

LogCorrespondenceMessageRequest.description: Request body for recording a correspondence message. The server assigns `id`, `recordedAt`, and any URN-shaped resource references the caller did not supply.

processId.description: Workflow process URN this correspondence belongs to.

workflowUrl.description: Workflow URI used to resolve the delivery sidecar.

templateRef.description: Delivery-sidecar entry-template id (inbound) or notification-template key (outbound).

direction.description: Direction of this message relative to the agency.

partyRole.description: Role of the party who sent or received this message.

producer.description: Producer family that authored or ingested this message.

summary.description: Required human-readable one-line summary of this correspondence event.

contentRef.description: Optional claim-check pointer to the message payload.

renderingId.description: Optional rendering URN this message was produced from.

actorRef.description: Optional actor reference responsible for sending or recording this message.

occurredAt.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp when the correspondence event occurred.

RenderCorrespondenceRequest.description: Request body for `POST /api/v1/correspondence/renderings`. Renders an outbound delivery-sidecar notification template with placeholder substitution against the supplied closed-shape variable set. Folds in the legacy `POST /api/v1/notifications/{workflowUrl}/render` route per ADR 0082 D-15 step 2.

workflowUrl.description: Workflow URI used to resolve the delivery sidecar's `notifications.templates` map.

templateRef.description: Notification template key from the delivery sidecar's `notifications.templates`.

processId.description: Optional workflow process URN. When present the server MAY enrich rendering variables from case state per the workflow's delivery sidecar.

variables.description: Caller-supplied rendering variables. Closed item shape; no `Record<string, unknown>` escape hatch.

BusinessCalendarDate.description: Single calendar-day entry returned by the business-calendar lookup endpoint (delivery sidecar §business-calendar; specs/sidecars/business-calendar.md §3 work week + §4 holiday schedule). Carries the date, its closed-no-extension day kind, and the optional holiday name. Lets a portal display "is today a business day?" or "what's the next business day?" without re-implementing the workWeek + holidays composition logic.

date.description: ISO 8601 date this entry classifies. Interpreted in the calendar's declared `timezone` (specs/sidecars/business-calendar.md §2.1 timezone).

dayKind.description: Closed-no-extension classification of the day. `business` matches the §3.3 evaluation (in `workWeek` AND not in `holidays`); `holiday` is in `holidays` regardless of `workWeek` (§4); `weekend` is outside `workWeek` and not declared as a holiday. Closed by design — adding a kind requires a schema major bump.

holidayName.description: Human-readable holiday name from the calendar's `holidays[].name` (specs/sidecars/business-calendar.md §4.1). Present iff `dayKind == "holiday"`. Open prose.

observed.description: True when this entry is the observed-date for a holiday whose nominal date falls on a non-working day (specs/sidecars/business-calendar.md §4.3). Absent or false on non-observed entries.

BusinessCalendarDateList.description: Cursor-paginated business-calendar date page (ADR 0082 D-7). Returned by `GET /api/v1/correspondence/calendar/{calendarId}/dates?from=...&to=...`. Days are returned in chronological order by `date` ascending. Calendars with thousands of entries (multi-year ranges) page; small ranges fit a single response.

calendarId.description: Calendar identifier this page belongs to (matches the `{calendarId}` path parameter and the delivery sidecar's calendar block).

timezone.description: IANA timezone the calendar declares (specs/sidecars/business-calendar.md §2.1). Surfaced once on the page envelope so consumers do not need to re-resolve the calendar block to interpret day boundaries.

items.description: Date entries in this page, ordered by `date` ascending. Each entry classifies one calendar day in the requested `from`..`to` range.

cursor.description: Opaque token for the next page. Omitted when hasMore is false.

hasMore.description: True when another page is available.

CorrespondenceRendering.description: Result of rendering a delivery-sidecar notification template. Renderings are server-stamped, content-addressable artifacts; producing one does not by itself send a correspondence message. A subsequent `POST /api/v1/correspondence` may reference this rendering's URN via `renderingId` to commit it as a sent outbound message.

workflowUrl.description: Workflow URI whose delivery sidecar supplied the template.

templateRef.description: Notification template key the renderer resolved.

category.description: Template category resolved from the delivery sidecar (drives due-process validation for adverse-decision notices).

renderedAt.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp when the rendering was produced.

subject.description: Rendered subject line. Empty templates without subjects produce a server-supplied default (e.g., the template description); the field is always present so consumers do not branch on absence.

sections.description: Ordered rendered sections after condition evaluation.

deliveryChannels.description: Delivery channels declared by the source template, projected through the closed `DeliveryChannelKind` enum.

rendered.description: Concatenated full-document rendering (sections joined with newlines) for clients that only need a single body string. Always present even when sections are empty post-condition (renders to the empty string).

items.description: Name of a template placeholder variable that was resolved during rendering.

resolvedVariables.description: Sorted, deduplicated names of placeholder variables resolved during rendering. Closed shape — only variable names, never values.

items.description: Name of a template placeholder variable required by the template but not supplied or resolvable from case state.

missingVariables.description: Sorted, deduplicated names of required template variables that were not resolved. When non-empty the rendering is advisory only and MUST NOT be referenced by a subsequent `LogCorrespondenceMessageRequest`; the server returns `WOS-1422` if a caller attempts to do so.
